title: Escape from Balis-Baurgh
content:

"'''Escape from Balis-Baurgh'''" is a short story written by [[Paul Balsamo]] with illustrations by [[Doug Shuler]]. It was published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' ''1'']] in [[February]] [[1994]] by [[West End Games]].

It tells the story of an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] called [[Grael]] and a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] scout called [[Junas Turner]] who are captured by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and sent to the fully automated prison colony of [[Balis-Baurgh]]. The short story is notable for being narrated in first person from the perspective of an Ewok who has no understanding of advanced technology.

==Plot summary==
[[Grael]] the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] tells a story to his son [[Ponto]] before bed. In this story Grael goes hunting for a [[Yootak]], and instead discovers a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] member, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Junas Turner]]. At first thinking Junas was just a particularly intelligent animal, Grael convinces him to follow back to the [[Tribe Panshee village]]. Before they can arrive, however, they are captured by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]].

The rebel and the Ewok are taken to [[Balis-Baurgh]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison/Legends|prison]]. Although overwhelmed by all that he sees at first, Grael slowly begins to learn [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and understand the workings of the prison, which is mostly automated by [[Droid/Legends|droids]] under the command of a [[4XB Programmer Droid]]. Grael is introduced to two more captured rebels, [[Lon (Rebel)|Lon]] and [[Becker]], and becomes convinced that Tribe [[Panshee]] should support "[[Tribe Rebellion]]" against "[[Tribe Empire]]." Junas tells Grael that the Empire is building a terrible [[Death Star II/Legends|weapon]] near [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], and that he must get news of it back to the Rebellion.

Grael is able to duplicate the command tones of the 4XB using his [[Flute/Legends|pan flute]], and escape with Junas, but they are forced to leave Lon and Becker behind in order to get news of the Death Star to the Rebel Alliance. They deliver the information to the Rebels, and return to Endor in time for the victory celebration.

Grael ends his story to his son, and heads to bed. The following morning, he and Junas will attempt to rescue Lon and Becker.

==Continuity==
Published nine years after [[Heir to the Empire|''Heir to the Empire'']] ([[1991]]), this short story paves the way for [[Talon Karrde]] to find the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]], his headquarters in that novel.<ref name="TERC" />
